title: Schivereckia doerfleri
intro: Schivereckia doerfleri, also known as the Alpine Phlox, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the family Polemoniaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in the treatment of various health issues such as wounds, infections, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Schivereckia doerfleri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in ointments and creams for topical application.
taxonomy: Schivereckia doerfleri is a species of flowering plant. It belongs to the family Papaveraceae, commonly known as the poppy family. The genus Schivereckia is a small group of plants native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. Schivereckia doerfleri is a perennial herb with a delicate appearance and white or pinkish flowers. The classification of Schivereckia doerfleri also places it within the order Ranunculales.
